surfaces.golden: 186 vectors — 7 canonicalize + 5 decimal + 5 signing + 16 dacs1 + 2 addressing + 4 bundle + 17 dispute/disclosure (8 dispute + 9 disclosure) + 36 settlement + 47 verify + 24 vet + 11 negotiate + 12 governance; byte-stable and reference-verifier-accepted.

cases.summary: object members ordered by UTF-16 code unit of key
nested objects sorted; array order preserved
only JCS-required escapes are applied
forward slash and non-ASCII are not escaped
safe integers serialise as plain decimals
non-integer numbers are rejected
the signed scope excludes the signature field
1.50 / 01.5 / 1.500 all canonicalise to 1.5
exponent / sign-plus / non-numeric are rejected
economically-equal amounts share a content hash; raw strings do not
amount MUST be > 0
a dacs-1-listing signature verifies under its own separator
mutating the signed scope breaks verification
SIG-2: a listing signature does not verify as a bundle signature
the domain-separator registry is the closed set of 16
SIG-4: DACS-X separators are dacs-x-* and disjoint from the §7.7 registry
OBSERVATION DACS-VERIFY-0001 (adjacent issue #42): a cci-lei: claim does NOT satisfy a bare lei requirement
0001 is naming-only: cci-lei: satisfies a cci-lei requirement
step 3b: an unverified presentedBy claim is rejected even if another verified claim satisfies the requirement
freshness Option A: absent timestamps and expired expiresAt fail before maxAge; expiresAt-only can pass unless maxAge needs issuedAt
SIWD Resource binds to lowercase hex of the full domain-separated signed_bytes
IT-1: verified-and-fresh authority-issued claim derives institutional
IT-1: verified-and-fresh non-authority claim derives verified
IT-1: raw key with no verifiedBy derives self-declared
IT-2: self-asserted identityTier is ignored and recomputed
IT-3: verified institutional claim wins over verified non-authority claim
IT-3: stale verifiedBy does not elevate an authority-issued claim
IT-3: malformed or unresolved verifiedBy does not elevate an authority-issued claim
an intake-only listing (no pay phase) may omit acceptedRails
step 8: a pay-* phase without acceptedRails fails
an expired validity window is rejected
OBSERVATION DACS-VERIFY-0003: spec rule yields stor-<64hex> (Demos uses stor-<40hex>; verify on substrate before relying)
reserved delimiters (: ? & = %) are percent-encoded with uppercase hex, and no other bytes are guessed
listing logical address encodes sellerPrimaryClaim + listingId variable segments before assembly
CM-4: the four §7.5.1 outcomes classify; an unknown decision throws
CM-5: VerifyResult.method must equal the producing kind — match accepted, mismatch rejected
CM-1/CM-3: a VerifyResult missing/invalid decision or method is rejected, never silently passed
CM-2: attestation anchors at dacs2:{jobId}:{scheme}:{identifier}:v{recipeVersion} (scheme lowercased per CF-2)
dacs2 attestation address encodes a colon-bearing identifier segment
dacs2 composite address encodes a colon-bearing evaluatedParty segment
VP-R1: decision=error + retryClass=transient + attempts<budget → retry
VP-R1: decision=error + transient at attempts==budget → no retry
VP-R3: decision=error + retryClass=permanent → never retry within session
VP-R4: indeterminate + retryOnIndeterminate unset (default false) → no retry
VP-R4: indeterminate + retryOnIndeterminate=true → retry
a terminal authority answer (pass/fail) is never retried
MA-1: a missing required claim → REJECT
MA-1: oneOf satisfied by any member → continue; satisfied by none → REJECT
MA-2: presentedBy scheme != primaryClaimSelector → REJECT
MA-3: selector set + presentedBy verifiedBy resolves decision!=pass → REJECT (tier-laundering guard)
MA-3 full resolution: dacs1 presence-only ACCEPTS a present-but-failing verifiedBy; vetMatch REJECTS it
MA-3: selector set + presentedBy resolves to pass → ACCEPT
find_claim: a verificationRequired claim whose verifiedBy resolves to indeterminate is treated as absent
vetFindClaim applies the same freshness pre-gate before maxAge after resolving verifiedBy decision
oneOf within-group precedence: error > indeterminate > fail
oneOf within-group precedence: indeterminate > fail when no error/pass is present
cross-accumulator precedence across required + oneOf: fail > error > indeterminate
VPC-4/R8-E: counterparty-malformed oneOf alternative keeps decision=error but attributes phaseSummary.errorClass=counterparty
price-band [bandCenter×(100−minPct)/100, ×(100+maxPct)/100] inclusive; edges accept, just-outside reject (CD-1 full precision)
a cross-currency agreement is rejected before any amount comparison
terms.rail not in listing.acceptedRails → REJECT
deliverable conformance: deliverableType + canonical hash + schemaUrl must all match the listing offering.deliverable
deadline ≤ committedAt + deadlineSecAfterCommit, measured against the ANCHORED committedAt (not generatedAt)
listing.validity.notAfter < committedAt → REJECT (listing expired between read and commit)
derivedFromPattern != listing pipeline pattern → REJECT
PS-3: a fixed-price agreement over negotiable pricing MUST equal bandCenter exactly, not merely lie within the band
priceAnchor absent MUST NOT cause rejection — an otherwise-valid agreement is accepted
when present, priceAnchor.price MUST be CD-1 canonical + attestationRef.contentHash present; non-canonical → REJECT
terms.price.amount MUST be CD-1 canonical — a non-canonical amount ("100.00") is rejected before comparison, not normalized-then-accepted (consistent with the §14.4 settlement lane)
GOV-2: the three §7.4.1 anchoring phases classify; an unknown phase throws
GOV-3 core: `in-code` (PA-1) is NOT canonically anchored; single-signer/multisig are
GOV-1 (advisory; `represents` is a verifier-internal hint, not a spec wire-field): a consumer surfacing a key + presenting PA-2 as single-steward → ACCEPT
GOV-1: a consumer that does not surface an authoritative signing key → REJECT
GOV-1: presenting a single-signer (PA-2) steward as a constituted multi-party body → REJECT
GOV-1: presenting a constituted body is honest only once the registry is actually multisig (PA-3)
GOV-3: a recipeVersion pinned at single-signer (PA-2) is evaluated against PIN-TIME phase, NOT the current multisig (PA-3) registry — append-only re-anchoring MUST NOT retro-upgrade a pin
GOV-3: a pinned `in-code` recipeVersion evaluates as not canonically anchored
GOV-3: a single-signer pin under a consumer trust floor of multisig → REJECT
GOV-3: a multisig pin meeting a multisig trust floor → ACCEPT
GOV-3 fail-closed: an unrecognised pin-time phase (malformed governance.anchoring) is REJECTED, not treated as canonically anchored
GOV-1 fail-closed: an unrecognised actualPhase is REJECTED before any disclosure decision
DACS-VERIFY-0004 completed AttestationBundle verifies with buyer + seller signatures
HTLC-9 AttestationBundle verifies and maps settlement-atomicity failure to failed-counterparty with destination reveal txRef
completed bundle missing a required seller signature → FAIL
wrong-length resolved public key → ERROR, not a false-negative FAIL
consistent dispute → PASS, disputed contribution reweighted to 0 (prior preserved)
an open dispute with no outcome yet → INDETERMINATE
a dispute-record verified against the wrong key → FAIL
an unparseable (non-32-byte) key → ERROR, not a false-negative FAIL
a post-hoc arbitration-rule swap (rule-ref mismatch) → FAIL
an outcome signed by the wrong key → FAIL
an outcome with a non-CD-1 refund amount → FAIL
a dispute pinned to an unknown bundle → FAIL
all channel members co-sign → transcript disclosure to the named arbitrator authorized
the credentialed arbitrator orders disclosure → authorized
DP-1 named-arbitrator-only: a recipient not in the agreed arbitrator allow-set → FAIL
DP-1 anti-swap: an agreed rule whose hash ≠ the record's pinned ruleRef → FAIL (can't swap in a rule naming an attacker's recipient)
a grant pinning a different transcript hash → FAIL (the disclosed transcript can't be swapped)
party-agreement missing a member's consent → FAIL (full consent required)
a grant not bound to the DisputeRecord → FAIL
a non-32-byte member key → ERROR, not a false-negative FAIL
a transcript with an unverifiable member signature → FAIL (authenticity)
pay-evm-erc20 success evidence passes PC-1..PC-6 + dacs-4-evidence signature
deliver-storage-program success evidence passes with deliverable anchor and no settlementFinality
amount.currency not resolved by rail.asset and handler settled → FAIL
success payment evidence missing settlementFinality → FAIL
delivery evidence carrying settlementFinality → FAIL
ok:true with errorClass present → FAIL
ok:false without errorClass → FAIL
result.attestationRef id not at expected dacs4 payment anchor → FAIL
dacs4 payment evidence address encodes a colon-bearing railId while phaseIndex stays structural
result.attestationRef contentHash not equal evidenceHash(evidence) → FAIL
failure evidence without non-empty reason → FAIL
resolved key is well-formed but not the signing key → FAIL
resolved signer key is not 32 bytes → ERROR
signer key cannot be resolved → INDETERMINATE
evidence.phase (pay-solana-spl) ≠ pinned rail.phaseHandler (pay-evm-erc20) → FAIL
handler-return txRefs ≠ signed evidence.paymentTxRefs → FAIL (signature covers paymentTxRefs only)
non-canonical PriceTerm.amount ("1.50") → FAIL (CD-1 minimal-form)
non-positive PriceTerm.amount ("0") → FAIL (amount MUST be > 0)
attestationRef.kind ≠ dacs-4-evidence (mislabelled as dacs-5-bundle) → FAIL
evidence signed by a non-orchestrator claim (key resolves) ≠ expected orchestrator → FAIL
success payment evidence omitting paymentTxRefs → FAIL (audit value)
success payment evidence omitting paymentAmount → FAIL (actual settled amount required)
deliver-storage-program success omitting deliverableContentHash/anchor → FAIL
delivery deliverableContentHash not 64-hex ("not-a-hash") → FAIL (content-addressing)
deliver-storage-program anchored at dacs4:entitlement namespace → FAIL (phase-specific anchor)
negative paymentFee ("-1") → FAIL (a fee may be 0, never negative)
paymentInput.amount (1 USDC) ≠ agreement.terms.price (5 USDC) → FAIL (underpayment)
rail railType evm-erc20 with phaseHandler pay-solana-spl → FAIL (incoherent rail)
evm-erc20 rail with a solana network → FAIL (railType↔asset/network coherence)
HTLC rail pins timelockSourceSec/timelockDestSec/sourceFinalitySec/safetyWindowSec and satisfies source-margin inequality → PASS
HTLC rail missing required sourceFinalitySec → FAIL
HTLC rail missing required safetyWindowSec → FAIL
HTLC source timelock not greater than dest timelock + source finality + safety → FAIL
cross-chain payment with irreversible foreign-chain value movement may return ok:true + txRefs while SR-2 anchoring is pending
rail with matching KINDS but asset.chainId≠network.chainId → PASS (RD-5 binds kinds, not chainId-equality)
delivery evidence carrying a non-settlementFinality payment field → PASS (SIG-5 preserve-unknown / open-world)
buyer bundle address is stor-{sha256(jobId + "-bundle-buyer")}
buyer/seller/orchestrator addresses are role-specific
dacs5 rating address encodes a colon-bearing rater ClaimReference
two-sided lookup fetches both expected addresses
two-sided lookup preserves a one-sided fetch result
two-sided lookup returns no sides when both addresses are absent
no bundles at either expected address → absent
both present and bundleHash-equal → unified
one present side → missing side aborted-by-self; present side aborted-by-other
canonically divergent bundles are retained for per-party policy
draft → vet-pending is legal
vet-pending → aborted-by-self is legal
settle-completed → finalised is legal
substrate-failure-paused may resume to a pending phase
commit-completed → negotiate-pending is illegal
finalised → rate-pending is illegal
substrate-failure-paused → finalised is illegal
settle-pending → settle-asymmetric (HTLC-9 dest-revealed-source-unclaimed open state) is legal
settle-asymmetric resolves forward: → settle-completed (htlc-claim in window) or → settle-failed (window expiry)
settle-asymmetric may pause while anchoring the :resolved record and resume to settle-asymmetric
settle-asymmetric is non-terminal: no direct → finalised, and no bundle outcome until it resolves
finalised → completed
permanent and exhausted transient failures → failed-perm
counterparty and settlement-atomicity failures → failed-counterparty
failed-substrate → failed-substrate
abort terminals map directly
non-terminal or unmapped phase error has no bundle outcome
failed-substrate is excluded from party_fault_denom
denominator zero returns null metrics, never zero
window filtering excludes out-of-window bundles from scoped count
windowingBasis is recorded and bundleRefs are ascending contentHash; re-derivation from bundleRefs reproduces metrics + count
L3 leaves rating/volume resolution unset without a resolver
a fetched bundle whose embedded jobId ≠ the looked-up jobId is ignored → absent (cross-session replay/misreturn)
two-sided reconciliation: victim scored over BOTH copies of one abort → self_copy wins → exactly one aborted-by-other, counted once (no double-count)
the WITHDRAWER scored over both copies → its own aborted-by-self self_copy → the aborter takes the hit (no counterparty fault)
withdrawer scored over ONLY the victim's counterparty-anchored copy → perspective_flip(aborted-by-other)=aborted-by-self → withdrawer still takes the hit
guard (i): a single-signed seller-anchored failed-counterparty copy is dropped before perspective_flip can depress the buyer's score
guard (ii): contradictory self/counterparty copies are excluded from BOTH numerator and party_fault_denom
guard (iii): orchestrator-anchored copies are evidence-only and never chosen as the counterparty perspective
R4-B fix: a party scored over only counterparty-anchored bundles IS scored via perspective_flip, NOT excluded (pre-R4-B anchoredBy-only narrowing was the defect)
ratings via resolver: (rater,jobId,targetRole) de-dup last-writer-wins by ratedAt (5 over 3) + no self-rating → averageBuyerRating 5
observedTransactionalVolume sums agreement.terms.price by currency over reconciled bundles whose agreementRef resolves (5 × 5 usdc = 25)
role_of_party is the externally-known binding, not self-declared `parties`: a buyer bundle that relabels its own claim still reads aborted-by-self literally (NOT flipped to a counterparty fault)

per-job role binding: a party that is buyer in one session and seller in another reads BOTH self-aborts literally — no spurious counterparty fault from a derivation-wide role; the off-role copy is a real signed seller-anchored bundle that verifies (honest fixture, not a mutated copy)
a bundle at the BUYER address NOT signed by the expected buyer claim is rejected → absent (anchoring binds to the externally-known party, NOT to self-declared/relabelled `parties` roles)
a present side whose role signature does NOT verify (wrong/forged key) is not classified present → absent (no abort provenance from unverifiable storage)
the finalisedAt window is closed-interval inclusive on BOTH ends — a bundle at exactly windowStart==windowEnd is scoped
